channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ivory Coast votes as Ouattara seeks a fourth term with key rivals sidelined
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — Voters in Ivory Coast cast ballots Saturday to elect a new leader as longtime President Alassane Ouattara seeks a fourth term, while his key contenders were barred from the race.
item.dc:creator: OPE ADETAYO and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Company recalls 2 million pounds of barbecue pork jerky that may contain metal wire
item.description: (The Associated Press) -- A South Dakota company is recalling more than 2.2 million pounds (998,000 kilograms) of Korean barbecue pork jerky sold at Costco and Sam's Club stores because the product may be contaminated with pieces of metal, federal health officials said Friday. LSI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, KREX 5

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?' New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Family matriarch dies day after rescue from remote island that saw family swim ashore to escape boat fire
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard rescued a family from Naushon Island after their boat caught fire, forcing them to swim to safety from Martha's Vineyard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thailand's Queen Mother Sirikit has died at age 93
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand’s Queen Mother Sirikit, who supervised royal projects to help the rural poor, preserve traditional craft-making and protect the environment, died on Friday. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: DENIS D. GRAY, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and TIAN MACLEOD JI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Anti-abortion pregnancy centers are looking to offer much more than ultrasounds and diapers
item.description: More anti-abortion pregnancy centers have been offering medical services such as testing for sexually transmitted infections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: Polling places open Saturday for in-person voting in two of the year’s most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor’s race and the contest to pick New Jersey’s next governor.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump speaks at tele-rally to boost GOP's chances in governor's race in New Jersey
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump urged his supporters in New Jersey late Friday to cast a ballot for the Republican candidate for governor, Jack Ciattarelli, before early voting starts in the state on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inspector Clouseau? The mystery man in an AP photo after the Louvre jewel heist creates a buzz
item.description: PARIS (AP) — It was shortly after the stunning heist of the crown jewels at the Louvre when Paris-based Associated Press photographer Thibault Camus caught in his frame a dapperly dressed young man walking by uniformed French police officers, their car blocking one of the museum gates.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Reagan ad that infuriated Trump set to run during World Series
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said on Friday that the anti-tariffs advertisement featuring former President Reagan, which angered President Trump, will air during the World Series before being paused on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, Fox 5

item.title: Girl shot in Minneapolis church returns home from the hospital
item.description: A 12-year-old girl who was shot in the head during a deadly attack at a Minneapolis Catholic church in August was released from the hospital Thursday and greeted with cheers as the police chief paraded her around town in a stretch limousine, according to the Minneapolis Star Tribune.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, pledged Friday to further embrace his Muslim identity in response to growing attacks by former Gov. Andrew Cuomo and his surrogates that he characterized as “racist and baseless.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 7 charged in 2024 Pennsylvania voter registration fraud that prosecutors say was motivated by money
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A yearlong investigation into suspected fraudulent voter registration forms submitted ahead of last year's presidential election produced criminal charges Friday against six street canvassers and the man who led their work in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: Hegseth’s order comes as the Trump administration has been striking alleged drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOJ prepares to send election monitors to California, New Jersey following requests from state GOPs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Department of Justice is preparing to send federal election observers to California and New Jersey next month, targeting two Democratic states holding off-year elections following requests from state Republican parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US says it now plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia as soon as Oct. 31
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The U.S. government plans to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, and could do so as early as Oct. 31, according to a Friday court filing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York adds $11M to hunger programs amid SNAP cuts, government shutdown
item.description: Governor Kathy Hochul announced $11 million in state funding for emergency food relief programs and urged the USDA to release $700 million in SNAP benefits to prevent a public health crisis, while the Fiscal Policy Institute suggested the state use its budget surplus to backfill the lost benefits.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US coffee prices spike due to tariffs and poor weather
item.description: It’s getting more expensive for Americans to get their caffeine fix. The average U.S. price of a pound of ground coffee hit $9.14 in September, a 3% increase from the August average of $8.87 and 41% higher than in September 2024, according to U.S. government figures.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN and CHRIS RUGABER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's what the White House lost with the East Wing's demolition
item.description: The East Wing's razing was met with criticism both by preservationists and by over half of the American public.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PHOTOS: Thieves cut through roof, steal hundreds of shoes in Florida mall heist
item.description: Florida authorities are searching for suspects who investigators said pulled off an elaborate heist to steal thousands of dollars in shoes from a Martin County mall this week.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KRON4

item.title: Inflation hit 3 percent in September as energy prices squeezed households
item.description: Annual inflation rose to 3% in September as a jump in gasoline and energy prices pushed price growth to its highest level since January.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security Administration announces 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment
item.description: Social Security and Supplemental Security Income (SSI) benefits will rise 2.8 percent in 2026 after the annual cost-of-living adjustment (COLA), the federal government announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shots fired, driver hurt and held after truck rams into Coast Guard base in California
item.description: Law enforcement officers have fired shots at a vehicle that backed into a U.S. Coast Guard base in the San Francisco Bay Area that had been the site of protests against federal immigration agents.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alaska Airlines resumes operations after an IT outage grounded its flights for hours
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it had to ground its planes for hours because of an information technology outage.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NBA head coach and player charged in sprawling sports betting and Mafia-backed poker schemes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers and a player for the Miami Heat were arrested Thursday along with more than 30 other people in a takedown of two sprawling gambling operations that authorities said leaked inside information about NBA athletes and rigged poker games backed
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, TIM REYNOLDS and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alabama executes man with nitrogen gas for 1993 murder over $200 drug debt
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted of helping to burn a man alive in 1993 over a $200 drug debt was executed by nitrogen gas on Thursday. Anthony Boyd, 54, was pronounced dead at 6:33 p.m. at William C. Holman Correctional Facility, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4 found dead in apartment of apparent overdoses were softball teammates
item.description: Four members of the same softball team were found dead from apparent drug overdoses inside an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 53 percent disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House to make room for his $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How inflatable costumes ballooned at anti-Trump rallies due to a protester's frog outfit
item.description: Puffed-out costumes — hippos in tutus, Mr. Potato Heads, dinosaurs — have quickly become a feature of protests against President Donald Trump’s administration, including the massive "No Kings" marches across the U.S. last weekend.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE looking to hire over 40 health care workers
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is hiring for more than 40 medical positions in its immigration detention centers across the nation.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and R.J. RICO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bodycam footage of Jay Cutler's arrest released
item.description: Cutler, 41, was arrested in October 2024 after police responded to a minor traffic collision where he had rear-ended another vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KRON4

item.title: Medicare agency recalling thousands of furloughed staffers for open enrollment
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) is temporarily calling back all furloughed staff starting next week, a spokesperson confirmed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Cheryl Hines says RFK Jr. suggested they fake separation during 2024 campaign
item.description: Cheryl Hines, the wife of Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., said her husband suggested they fake separating during his 2024 presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, Fox 5
